read_file({"path": "internal/engine/language_tiers.go"}) → package engine

import "fmt"

// Tier represents the analysis capability tier for a programming language.
type Tier int

const (
	// TierNone means the language is recognized but no analysis is available.
	TierNone Tier = iota

	// TierGeneric means only generic (regex, line-based) analysis is available.
	TierGeneric

	// TierLint means AST-level linting is available but no type-aware analysis.
	TierLint

	// TierParse means the language can be parsed with type information available.
	TierParse

	// TierFull means full type-aware analysis including call-graph, dataflow, etc.
	TierFull
)

var tierNames = map[Tier]string{
	TierNone:    "None",
	TierGeneric: "Generic",
	TierLint:    "Lint",
	TierParse:   "Parse",
	TierFull:    "Full",
}

// String returns a human-readable name for the tier.
func (t Tier) String() string {
	if name, ok := tierNames[t]; ok {
		return name
	}
	return fmt.Sprintf("Tier(%d)", int(t))
}

// languageTiers maps language identifiers to their analysis capability tier.
// Every language discovered by the extension registry must have an entry here.
var languageTiers = map[string]Tier{
	// TierFull: languages with type-aware, call-graph, and dataflow analysis.
	"go":     TierFull,
	"ts":     TierFull,
	"typescript": TierFull,
	"js":     TierFull,
	"javascript": TierFull,
	"py":     TierFull,
	"python": TierFull,
	"rs":     TierFull,
	"rust":   TierFull,

	// TierGeneric: languages with basic structural analysis only.
	"rb":      TierGeneric,
	"ruby":    TierGeneric,
	"java":    TierGeneric,
	"kt":      TierGeneric,
	"kotlin":  TierGeneric,
	"sh":      TierGeneric,
	"bash":    TierGeneric,
	"c":       TierGeneric,
	"cpp":     TierGeneric,
	"c++":     TierGeneric,
	"cs":      TierGeneric,
	"csharp":  TierGeneric,
	"c#":      TierGeneric,
	"swift":   TierGeneric,
	"php":     TierGeneric,
	"elixir":  TierGeneric,
	"erlang":  TierGeneric,
	"scala":   TierGeneric,
	"lua":     TierGeneric,
	"r":       TierGeneric,
	"zig":     TierGeneric,
	"sql":     TierGeneric,
	"proto":   TierGeneric,
	"protobuf": TierGeneric,
}

// TierForLanguage returns the analysis tier for the given language identifier.
// Unknown languages default to TierGeneric.
func TierForLanguage(lang string) Tier {
	if tier, ok := languageTiers[lang]; ok {
		return tier
	}
	return TierGeneric
}

read_file({"path": "internal/engine/language_tiers_test.go"}) → package engine

import (
	"testing"
)

func TestTierString(t *testing.T) {
	tests := []struct {
		tier Tier
		want string
	}{
		{TierNone, "None"},
		{TierGeneric, "Generic"},
		{TierLint, "Lint"},
		{TierParse, "Parse"},
		{TierFull, "Full"},
		{Tier(99), "Tier(99)"},
	}

	for _, tt := range tests {
		got := tt.tier.String()
		if got != tt.want {
			t.Errorf("Tier(%d).String() = %q, want %q", int(tt.tier), got, tt.want)
		}
	}
}

func TestTierForLanguage_KnownLanguages(t *testing.T) {
	// Verify every known language ID in the registry returns the expected tier.
	// This test breaks when a language is added to discovery but not registered here.
	tests := []struct {
		lang string
		want Tier
	}{
		// TierFull
		{"go", TierFull},
		{"ts", TierFull},
		{"typescript", TierFull},
		{"js", TierFull},
		{"javascript", TierFull},
		{"py", TierFull},
		{"python", TierFull},
		{"rs", TierFull},
		{"rust", TierFull},

		// TierGeneric
		{"rb", TierGeneric},
		{"ruby", TierGeneric},
		{"java", TierGeneric},
		{"kt", TierGeneric},
		{"kotlin", TierGeneric},
		{"sh", TierGeneric},
		{"bash", TierGeneric},
		{"c", TierGeneric},
		{"cpp", TierGeneric},
		{"c++", TierGeneric},
		{"cs", TierGeneric},
		{"csharp", TierGeneric},
		{"c#", TierGeneric},
		{"swift", TierGeneric},
		{"php", TierGeneric},
		{"elixir", TierGeneric},
		{"erlang", TierGeneric},
		{"scala", TierGeneric},
		{"lua", TierGeneric},
		{"r", TierGeneric},
		{"zig", TierGeneric},
		{"sql", TierGeneric},
		{"proto", TierGeneric},
		{"protobuf", TierGeneric},
	}

	for _, tt := range tests {
		got := TierForLanguage(tt.lang)
		if got != tt.want {
			t.Errorf("TierForLanguage(%q) = %s, want %s", tt.lang, got, tt.want)
		}
	}
}

func TestTierForLanguage_UnknownDefaultsToGeneric(t *testing.T) {
	tests := []string{
		"", "unknown", "haskell", "ocaml", "fortran", "cobol", "dart", "groovy",
	}

	for _, lang := range tests {
		got := TierForLanguage(lang)
		if got != TierGeneric {
			t.Errorf("TierForLanguage(%q) = %s, want %s (default for unknown)", lang, got, TierGeneric)
		}
	}
}

func TestTierForLanguage_CaseSensitive(t *testing.T) {
	// The registry is case-sensitive; "Go" is not the same as "go".
	got := TierForLanguage("Go")
	if got != TierGeneric {
		t.Errorf("TierForLanguage(%q) = %s, want %s (case-sensitive)", "Go", got, TierGeneric)
	}

	got2 := TierForLanguage("GO")
	if got2 != TierGeneric {
		t.Errorf("TierForLanguage(%q) = %s, want %s (case-sensitive)", "GO", got2, TierGeneric)
	}
}

func TestLanguageTiersCoverage(t *testing.T) {
	// The 22 core language IDs from the plan must all be present.
	coreLanguages := []string{
		"go", "ts", "js", "py", "rs", // TierFull
		"rb", "java", "kt", "sh", "c", "cpp", "cs", "swift", "php",
		"elixir", "erlang", "scala", "lua", "r", "zig", "sql", "proto", // TierGeneric
	}

	for _, lang := range coreLanguages {
		if _, ok := languageTiers[lang]; !ok {
			t.Errorf("core language %q missing from languageTiers registry", lang)
		}
	}
}
